summary: This cluster examines the generation and characterization of high-cell-density (HCD) mutants in *Chlamydomonas reinhardtii* through continuous subculturing, a method that enriches insertional mutations linked to mitochondrial dysfunction. Key findings demonstrate that these mutants achieve significantly higher biomass production than wild-type strains, with specific isolates showing gain-of-function mutations identified via inverse PCR. The research focuses on laboratory-based experimental evolution rather than a specific geographic or ecological scope, emphasizing the mechanistic link between mitochondrial impairment and enhanced growth phenotypes.
